title: Abdominal pain
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped adult abdominal-pain identity and matching terms within DHA telehealth.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

red_flags.text: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped GI bleeding, vomiting, severe pain, fever, guarding or rebound, peritoneal, weight-loss, jaundice, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

red_flags.text: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped safety-netting and clinician-decided referral documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

investigations.text: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped onset, location, duration, character, radiation, meals or bowels, nausea or vomiting, bowel, urinary, fever, surgery, medication, presets, and structured history.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

investigations.text: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed blood, urinalysis, and imaging fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

source_limitations: Children and non-telehealth settings are outside the exact applicability statement.

Broad GI pain alias, bowel-sound and general vital fields, abdominal-mass label, previous-record review, generic clinician plan, generic follow-up, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, differential diagnosis, investigation selection or interpretation, treatment, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
